primary_accounting_issue: Eligibility of intragroup dividend receivable as a hedged item for foreign currency risk in consolidated financial statements under IFRS hedge accounting rules

primary_authority.reason: Generally prohibits hedge accounting for intragroup transactions in consolidated financial statements
Provides exception allowing foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items to qualify as hedged item in consolidated statements when functional currencies differ

supporting_authority.reason: Explains that intragroup monetary assets cannot be eliminated on consolidation without recording currency fluctuation effects, creating the FX exposure that may be hedged
Clarifies that hedge accounting for net investment applies only when net assets of foreign operation are included in financial statements

peripheral_authority.reason: Disclosures about hedge accounting are relevant but do not determine eligibility for hedge accounting treatment
Reduced disclosures for eligible entities do not alter the underlying hedge accounting eligibility rules

treatment_families.family: Hedge accounting for foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items
General prohibition on hedge accounting for intragroup items

approaches.label: Intragroup monetary item hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: Paragraph 6.3.6 of IFRS 9 explicitly permits designation of foreign currency risk of intragroup monetary items (including dividend receivables) as hedged items in consolidated financial statements when the intragroup item is between entities with different functional currencies and the FX risk affects consolidated profit or loss

approaches.label: Net investment hedge accounting
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IFRIC 16 clarifies that hedges of net investments in foreign operations may qualify for hedge accounting when the net assets of the foreign operation are included in consolidated financial statements
